evals.prompt: I just shipped a 'duplicate project' feature. Plan was: deep clone with new IDs, user-prompted naming, admin/owner-only permission, synchronous duplication, button on project detail page. Code is in. Can you review it?
evals.expected_output: Runs Full review: Layer 1 (plan alignment — checks all 5 decisions are reflected), Layer 2 (system integrity — boundaries, design system, code standards), Layer 3 (production readiness — error handling, edge cases, loading states). Reports findings with severity. Ends with Disposition asking what to do with each severity tier.
expectations: Picks Full review mode (or equivalent) since a plan exists
Reads the plan and checks each of the 5 decisions against what was built
Covers Layer 2 system integrity (architecture boundaries, design system, patterns)
Covers Layer 3 production readiness (error handling, edge cases, loading states)
Ends with a Disposition section that drives the next step (fix now, defer, log)

evals.prompt: I had a bug where the login redirect was wrong. We just fixed it (turned out to be a stale session cookie path). Can you do a quick post-fix review to make sure the fix didn't break anything else?
evals.expected_output: Picks Lite review mode (post-recover fix, no plan needed). Skips Layer 1. Focuses on Layer 2 (does the fix respect system boundaries?) and Layer 3 (does it handle edge cases like the original symptom?). Drives Disposition.
expectations: Picks Lite review (or equivalent — not full) since this is a post-fix check
Skips or short-circuits Layer 1 (no plan to compare against)
Checks Layer 2: does the fix introduce boundary violations or pattern drift?
Checks Layer 3: edge cases (what about other paths that might be stale?), error handling, regression risk
Drives Disposition: asks whether to fix related issues now or log them

evals.prompt: I dispatched 3 parallel subagents to build: (a) a new 'duplicates' table migration, (b) a POST /projects/:id/duplicate API endpoint, and (c) a 'Duplicate Project' UI button with a name modal. All 3 are done. Can you review the integrated result?
evals.expected_output: Picks Parallel review mode. Reviews each slice against its own contract first, then runs a cross-slice integration review (shared types between API and UI, no duplicate imports, no boundary violations, integration points wired correctly). Flags integration issues as Critical.
expectations: Reviews each slice (migration, API, UI) against its own contract first
Runs cross-slice integration review after per-slice pass
Checks shared types between API and UI slices
Checks for duplicate dependencies or boundary leaks between slices
Labels integration issues as Critical even if individual slices pass
